site.tagline: Compare HiFi speakers visually at true scale

meta.homeTitle: TrueScale — visually compare HiFi speakers at true scale
meta.homeDescription: Visually compare bookshelf and floorstanding HiFi speakers side by side, at their real-world dimensions. Curated specs from {brandCount} manufacturers.
meta.brandHomeDescription: {brand} catalog: {count} models compared visually at true scale, with full specs, dimensions and drivers side by side.
meta.brandsDescription: Every speaker brand we currently track plus the upcoming additions on the roadmap. From Monitor Audio and Dali to KEF, Sonus Faber and more.
meta.speakerDescriptionFallback: Full specs, dimensions and drivers for the {brand} {model}, plus side-by-side comparison with similar speakers at real-world scale.
meta.compare4Title: Compare 4 speakers at true scale — TrueScale
meta.compare4Description: Side-by-side visual comparison of up to four HiFi speakers, with full spec table — dimensions, drivers, frequency response and more.
meta.planDescription: Why TrueScale exists, what we curate, and how the catalog grows. A neutral comparator for HiFi enthusiasts.
meta.supportTitle: Support TrueScale — keep the catalog ad-free
meta.supportDescription: If TrueScale saved you a research session, a small donation keeps the catalog growing without sponsored rankings or ads.
meta.contactDescription: Send a message to the maker of TrueScale: corrections, brand suggestions, partnerships or anything else.

brandDescriptions.monitorAudio: British brand founded in 1972 in Cambridgeshire. Designs and builds its own aluminium-magnesium (C-CAM) cones and ribbon tweeters, featured across the Bronze, Silver, Gold and Platinum lines — long-standing references in both accessible and high-end HiFi.
brandDescriptions.dali: Danish brand founded in 1983 in Nørager. The name stands for «Danish Audiophile Loudspeaker Industries». Known for its hybrid soft-dome + ribbon tweeter modules and natural-wood enclosures across the Oberon, Rubicon and Epicon series.
brandDescriptions.dynaudio: Danish brand founded in 1977 in Skanderborg. Designs and manufactures its own MSP cones, the Esotar series of soft-dome tweeters and DDC directivity-control technology in-house — the hallmarks of the Confidence, Contour and Heritage lines.
brandDescriptions.wharfedale: British brand founded in 1932 in Ilkley, Yorkshire by Gilbert Briggs. One of the oldest names in HiFi, with a heritage spanning the Linton, Denton and Diamond classics and a current line — Aura, Elysian and Dovedale — that pairs woven glass-fibre cones with the in-house AMT tweeter.
brandDescriptions.dutchDutch: Dutch boutique active-monitor maker founded in 2014 by Martijn Mensink and Marcel Polman. Best known for the 8c — a sealed/cardioid three-way reference whose controlled-directivity DSP design crossed over from pro studios into high-end home listening rooms.
brandDescriptions.rizziAcoustics: Boutique loudspeaker maker behind The Owl — a Baltic-birch standmount built around a patented multi-vibration membrane that turns the entire front panel into the radiating surface, paired with a single driver inside a curved birch-plywood cabinet.
brandDescriptions.bowersWilkins: British high-end loudspeaker maker founded in Worthing in 1966 by John Bowers. The 800 Series Diamond is the flagship reference line — Solid Body Tweeter-on-Top with diamond dome, Continuum cone midranges and Aerofoil-cone bass drivers inside the Matrix-braced cabinet, a long-standing studio reference at Abbey Road.
brandDescriptions.kef: British high-end loudspeaker maker founded in 1961 by Raymond Cooke. KEF's defining technology is the Uni-Q point-source array — a tweeter sitting in the acoustic centre of the midrange cone — present from the LSX II and LS50 Meta up to the R Meta, Reference, Blade and Muon flagships.
brandDescriptions.focal: French high-end loudspeaker maker founded in 1979 by Jacques Mahul in Saint-Étienne. Defined by the pure-beryllium inverted dome tweeter and the patented 'W' sandwich-cone driver, with the Utopia line as the flagship — handcrafted multi-way columns that trickle down into the Sopra and Aria ranges.
brandDescriptions.sonusFaber: Italian high-end loudspeaker maker founded in 1980 by Franco Serblin in Arcugnano, near Vicenza. Defined by solid-wood cabinets shaped like the lute, leather grilles and the natural-fibre cone — from the Heritage line (Amator family) up to the Aida and Suprema flagships.
brandDescriptions.klipsch: American loudspeaker maker founded in 1946 by Paul W. Klipsch in Hope, Arkansas. Defined by horn-loaded Tractrix compression tweeters and copper Cerametallic woofers — high-efficiency, dynamic-leaning designs spanning the Reference Premiere up to the Heritage line (Cornwall, Forte, Heresy, La Scala, Klipschorn).
brandDescriptions.polk: American loudspeaker maker founded in 1972 by Matthew Polk and George Klopfer in Baltimore, Maryland. Known for the Pinnacle Ring Radiator tweeter, Turbine Cone driver and the Power Port bass-loading system — the Reserve line is the current audiophile-oriented flagship, sitting beneath the Legend L800 SDA reference.
brandDescriptions.canton: German loudspeaker maker founded in 1972 in Niederlauken (Weilrod). Defined by the Black Ceramic Tungsten (BCT) cone drivers and Mundorf crossovers — the Reference line is the audiophile flagship, topped by the Reference Alpha 1 as the state-of-the-art reference.
brandDescriptions.paradigm: Canadian loudspeaker maker founded in 1982 in Mississauga, Ontario. Defined by the AL-MAC ceramic tweeter, AL-MAG midrange cone and CARBON-X bass driver paired with Active Ridge Technology (ART) surrounds — the Founder line is the current audiophile flagship, sitting beneath the limited Persona reference.

compare.scaleDisclaimer: We do our best to source photos that match each speaker as faithfully as possible, but minor differences in framing, perspective and trim level mean the rendered proportions and the relative size between models can be approximate rather than exact.

compare4.empty: Pick at least one speaker to start.

specs.title: Specifications
specs.diffNote: Larger value highlighted in green. For low-frequency extension, lower Hz = greater bass reach, so the deeper side is highlighted.

support.title: Did this help your
support.story: Truescale was born out of curiosity — I wanted to know the real size of the gear I kept seeing online.
support.storyAlt: It's a personal project I maintain in my spare time, with the goal of keeping it clean — no invasive ads, no sponsored rankings — while continuously adding new brands. The comparator is neutral, and that's not changing.
support.storyClosing: Your support helps cover the domain costs and keeps the project moving forward.
support.tierCoffeeBlurb: Covers a month of the domain.
support.tierCDBlurb: Adds a brand-new manufacturer to the catalog.
support.tierRecordBlurb: Equals a full weekend curating data.
support.thanksBlurb: Every contribution goes directly back into the project. If you want this site to stay ad-free, this is the way.
support.roadmapBlurb: Upcoming brands and prioritized improvements. Donations accelerate this order.
